description: Kiln is a single reusable GitHub Action (datashaman/kiln-action@v1) that provides an AI-powered development pipeline. Users file an issue, and Kiln automatically triages it, writes a spec, implements it, reviews it, and ships it — all orchestrated through one action that routes internally based on GitHub event type and label state.

userStories.title: Action Entry Point & Project Scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer building Kiln, I want the foundational action structure (action.yml, TypeScript build pipeline, dist/ output) so that the action can be installed and invoked in any GitHub repo.
acceptanceCriteria: action.yml exists with runs.using: node24 and runs.main: dist/index.js
Single required input: anthropic_api_key
TypeScript source compiles to dist/ via ncc or equivalent bundler
package.json with build, test, and lint scripts
Action can be referenced as uses: datashaman/kiln-action@v1 in a workflow
Entry point (src/index.ts) reads the GitHub event payload and invokes the router

userStories.title: Event Router
userStories.description: As Kiln, I want to inspect the incoming GitHub event type, action, and label state to determine which pipeline stage to execute, so that one action handles all stages.
acceptanceCriteria: Router maps events to stages per these rules: issues.opened → triage; issue_comment.created (if issue has kiln:intake or kiln:needs-info) → re-triage; issues.labeled with kiln:specifying → specify; pull_request_review.submitted approved on PR with kiln:spec label → approve-spec; issues.labeled with kiln:implementing → implement; pull_request.opened or pull_request.synchronize on PR with kiln:implementation label → review; pull_request_review.submitted with changes_requested on PR with kiln:implementation label → fix; pull_request_review.submitted approved or check_suite.completed on PR with kiln:implementation label → release
Unmatched events result in a clean no-op exit (exit code 0, no error)
Router logs which stage it selected (or "no matching stage") for debugging
Router passes parsed context (issue number, PR number, labels, event payload) to the selected stage handler

userStories.title: Blocked Guard
userStories.description: As a repo maintainer, I want to add kiln:blocked to any issue or PR to immediately halt all Kiln automation on that item, so that I have an emergency stop.
acceptanceCriteria: Before executing any stage, the router checks if the relevant issue or PR has the kiln:blocked label
If blocked, Kiln posts a comment: "🔥 **Kiln** — Automation is blocked on this item. Remove kiln:blocked to resume."
If blocked, Kiln exits cleanly (exit code 0) without running the stage
The blocked check covers all stages: triage, re-triage, specify, approve-spec, implement, review, fix, release
For PR events, Kiln checks both the PR labels and the linked issue labels for kiln:blocked

userStories.title: Claude Code Action Integration Layer
userStories.description: As Kiln, I want a shared utility to invoke anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 with a prompt and receive structured output, so that all AI stages use a consistent integration pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Utility function accepts: prompt (string), anthropic API key, and optional configuration
Utility shells out to / invokes anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 passing the prompt via direct_prompt
Utility captures and returns the action's output
Utility handles timeouts (hard-coded 30 minutes default in v1)
Utility handles errors and surfaces them as issue/PR comments when possible
API key is passed securely (never logged or exposed in comments)

userStories.title: Stage 1 — Triage (Issue Opened)
userStories.description: As a repo maintainer, I want new issues to be automatically classified by type, complexity, and clarity so that the pipeline can proceed or request more information.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers on issues.opened
Sends issue title and body to Claude with a triage prompt
Claude responds with structured JSON: { type, complexity, clear_enough, comment, labels }
Kiln parses the JSON response and applies labels: type:{type}, size:{complexity}
If clear_enough is true: applies kiln:specifying label, posts confirmation comment
If clear_enough is false: applies kiln:needs-info label, posts clarification comment from Claude
Posts a Kiln-branded comment indicating triage result

userStories.title: Stage 1b — Re-triage (Issue Comment After Clarification)
userStories.description: As an issue author, I want Kiln to re-evaluate my issue after I provide clarification so that the pipeline can proceed.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers on issue_comment.created
Only runs if the issue has kiln:intake or kiln:needs-info label
Ignores comments from bots/actions (prevents infinite loops)
Sends full issue body + all comments to Claude for re-triage
If now clear enough: removes kiln:needs-info, applies kiln:specifying, posts confirmation
If still unclear: posts follow-up clarification request, keeps kiln:needs-info

userStories.title: Stage 2 — Specification Agent
userStories.description: As Kiln, I want to generate a detailed implementation spec from a triaged issue so that the implementation agent has clear instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers when issue receives kiln:specifying label
Checks out the repo and sends issue context + codebase access to Claude
Claude creates a spec file at specs/issue-{number}.md with sections: Overview, Requirements, Technical Design, Test Plan, Out of Scope, Implementation Notes
Claude commits the spec to branch kiln/spec/issue-{number} and pushes
Kiln opens a PR titled "📋 Kiln Spec: {issue title}" targeting main
PR body references the issue number and includes review instructions
PR is labeled with kiln:spec and needs-human-review
Issue is updated: kiln:specifying removed, kiln:spec-review added
Comment posted on issue linking to the spec PR

userStories.title: Stage 3 — Human Spec Approval Gate
userStories.description: As a repo maintainer, I want spec PRs to require my explicit approval before implementation begins, so that I control what gets built.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers on pull_request_review.submitted where state is approved
Only runs on PRs with the kiln:spec label
Merges the spec PR via squash merge
Extracts the issue number from the branch name (kiln/spec/issue-{number})
Applies kiln:implementing label to the linked issue
Removes kiln:spec-review label from the issue
Posts comment on the issue: implementation agent is starting

userStories.title: Stage 4 — Implementation Agent
userStories.description: As Kiln, I want to generate a full implementation (code + tests) from an approved spec so that the feature is built automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers when issue receives kiln:implementing label
Checks out the repo and reads the spec at specs/issue-{number}.md
Sends spec + codebase context to Claude with implementation prompt
Claude implements on branch kiln/impl/issue-{number}, following project conventions from CLAUDE.md
Claude writes all tests specified in the Test Plan
Claude runs tests and lint, fixing failures before committing
Uses conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.)
Kiln opens a PR titled "🔨 Kiln Impl: {issue title}" targeting main
PR body references spec file and includes Closes #{issue_number}
PR is labeled with kiln:implementation and needs-review
Issue is updated: kiln:implementing removed, kiln:in-review added
Comment posted on issue linking to the implementation PR

userStories.title: Stage 5 — AI Code Review
userStories.description: As Kiln, I want implementation PRs to be automatically reviewed by an AI reviewer so that code quality is validated before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers on pull_request.opened and pull_request.synchronize
Only runs on PRs with the kiln:implementation label
Checks out the repo with full history (fetch-depth: 0)
Claude reads the referenced spec and reviews the diff against acceptance criteria
Review covers: correctness, security, performance, error handling, test coverage, style consistency
If everything passes: Claude approves the PR
If issues found: Claude requests changes with specific inline comments and fix instructions
Review is posted as a proper GitHub PR review (not just comments)

userStories.title: Stage 6 — Fix Review Feedback
userStories.description: As Kiln, I want the implementation agent to automatically address review feedback so that the PR can be re-reviewed and approved.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers on pull_request_review.submitted where state is changes_requested
Only runs on PRs with the kiln:implementation label
Checks out the PR's head branch
Claude reads all review comments and inline feedback
Claude addresses each comment: fixes code, adds tests, resolves concerns
Claude commits fixes and pushes to the PR branch
Claude replies to each review comment explaining the changes made
The push triggers a new synchronize event, which triggers re-review (Stage 5)

userStories.title: Stage 7 — Auto-merge & Release
userStories.description: As Kiln, I want approved implementation PRs with green CI to be automatically merged and released so that the pipeline completes without manual intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers on pull_request_review.submitted (approved) and check_suite.completed
Only runs on PRs with the kiln:implementation label
Checks that the PR has at least one approval
Checks that all CI checks are passing (success or skipped)
If both conditions met: merges the PR via squash merge
Extracts issue number from PR body (Closes #N)
Applies kiln:done label to the issue
Closes the issue
Posts a final Kiln comment on the issue confirming release
If conditions not met: no-op (waits for remaining condition)

userStories.title: Label Definitions & Setup Documentation
userStories.description: As a user installing Kiln, I want clear documentation of the required labels and their hex colors so that I can set up my repo correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: README documents all required labels with names, descriptions, and hex colors: Pipeline state (kiln:intake, kiln:needs-info, kiln:specifying, kiln:spec-review, kiln:implementing, kiln:in-review, kiln:done, kiln:blocked), PR markers (kiln:spec, kiln:implementation, needs-human-review, needs-review), Type (type:feature, type:bug, type:improvement, type:chore), Size (size:xs, size:s, size:m, size:l, size:xl)
A setup script or GitHub Actions workflow is provided (or documented steps) to create all labels

userStories.title: Loop Prevention & Edge Case Handling
userStories.description: As Kiln, I want safeguards against infinite loops and race conditions so that the pipeline doesn't run away or corrupt state.
acceptanceCriteria: Bot/action comments are ignored in re-triage (prevents triage → comment → re-triage loop)
Label events triggered by Kiln itself don't cause duplicate stage runs (debounce or actor check)
If a stage fails, Kiln posts an error comment on the issue/PR and does not advance the pipeline
If a spec PR already exists for an issue, the specify stage does not create a duplicate
If an implementation PR already exists for an issue, the implement stage does not create a duplicate
Concurrent events for the same issue are handled gracefully (last-write-wins or queue)

userStories.title: Error Handling & Observability
userStories.description: As a repo maintainer, I want clear error reporting and logging so that I can diagnose pipeline failures.
acceptanceCriteria: All stages wrap execution in try/catch and post error details as issue/PR comments
Error comments are clearly branded (e.g. "🔥 **Kiln** — Error in {stage}: {message}")
GitHub Actions step outputs include the stage name, duration, and result
Failed stages do not advance the pipeline (no label changes on error)
Timeout errors produce a specific message indicating the stage timed out

userStories.title: README & Getting Started Guide
userStories.description: As a developer who wants to use Kiln, I want a clear README with install instructions and a usage guide so that I can set up the pipeline in my repo.
acceptanceCriteria: README includes: project overview, pipeline diagram, install steps, configuration reference
Install steps cover: adding ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret, creating labels, adding workflow file, adding CLAUDE.md
Example workflow file is provided (copy-paste ready)
Example CLAUDE.md template is provided with placeholder sections
Safety controls documented: kiln:blocked label, branch protections, timeouts
Troubleshooting section covers common issues (missing labels, missing secret, permissions)

userStories.title: Example Workflow File
userStories.description: As a user installing Kiln, I want a ready-to-use workflow file so that I can copy it into my repo and start using Kiln immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Example file provided at a documented path (e.g. in README or examples/)
Workflow listens on all required events: issues, issue_comment, pull_request, pull_request_review, check_suite
Workflow has correct permissions: issues: write, contents: write, pull-requests: write
Single job that runs datashaman/kiln-action@v1 with anthropic_api_key input
File includes comments explaining each trigger

userStories.title: CLAUDE.md Template for Kiln Users
userStories.description: As a user installing Kiln, I want a CLAUDE.md template that constrains all Kiln agents to safe behavior so that automation stays scoped and predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes sections: Project description, Conventions (language, test runner, linting, commit style), Rules for Kiln Agents
Default rules include: never modify workflows, never modify CLAUDE.md, always read spec before implementing, always run tests, keep changes scoped, comment on issue if blocked
Template is clearly marked as a starting point that users should customize
Provided in README and optionally as a standalone file (e.g. examples/CLAUDE.md)
